title: cornus stolonifera var. coloradensis
intro: Cornus stolonifera var. coloradensis, also known as Colorado dogwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Cornaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Colorado dogwood are said to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for its medicinal uses.
